# UC-10.13.14 · Network Session Anomaly (CIM)

## Description

Session count per host vs. baseline — lateral movement and port scans increase discrete sessions.

## Value

Session count per host vs. baseline — lateral movement and port scans increase discrete sessions.

## Implementation

Tune per server role; datacenter hosts expected higher `dsts`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Firewall session logs → All_Traffic.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `action` allowed/blocked with session identifiers.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Tune per server role; datacenter hosts expected higher `dsts`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=network tag=session
| bin _time span=10m
| stats dc(dest_port) as ports, dc(dest) as dsts by src, _time
| where ports>100 OR dsts>50
```

Understanding this SPL

**Network Session Anomaly (CIM)** — Session count per host vs. baseline — lateral movement and port scans increase discrete sessions.

Documented **Data sources**: `action` allowed/blocked with session identifiers. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Firewall session logs → All_Traffic. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where ports>100 OR dsts>50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports, dc(All_Traffic.dest) as dsts from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, _time span=10m
| where ports>100 OR dsts>50
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Network Session Anomaly (CIM)** — Session count per host vs. baseline — lateral movement and port scans increase discrete sessions.

Documented **Data sources**: `action` allowed/blocked with session identifiers. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Firewall session logs → All_Traffic. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Filters the current rows with `where ports>100 OR dsts>50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (noisy hosts), Scatter (ports vs. dsts).

## SPL

```spl
index=security tag=network tag=session
| bin _time span=10m
| stats dc(dest_port) as ports, dc(dest) as dsts by src, _time
| where ports>100 OR dsts>50
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports, dc(All_Traffic.dest) as dsts from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, _time span=10m
| where ports>100 OR dsts>50
```

## Visualization

Table (noisy hosts), Scatter (ports vs. dsts).
